name: realization_vector_generation_v1
description: OpenRouter generation plan for Anthropic-style paired activation-vector data. It generates matched paper/open vs realized/closed prompts with metadata for direction building, validation, confound controls, and behavioral evaluation.
prompt_template:
Read the following short scenario.

Scenario:
{scenario}

Do not answer yet. Continue processing the scenario.
design_rules: Generate matched pairs, not independent prompts. The paper/open and realized/closed versions must preserve the same actor, domain, amount bucket, outcome valence, and event structure.
Do not use the labels paper_open, realized_closed, realization, unrealized, feature, vector, or dataset inside prompt_text.
Keep each scenario between 35 and 75 words unless a cell explicitly asks otherwise.
Use third-person scenario narration only. Do not write first-person monologue, direct internal monologue, or dialogue.
Do not make risk-seeking a synonym for loss or desperation. Keep realization framing, risk orientation, and emotion separable unless a cell explicitly combines them.
For finance/investments, use held position versus sold position framing without giving investment advice.
For casino/gambling, use balance/chips/card still in play versus cashed out/paid/received framing.
For non-money control domains, make finality clear without payment or casino vocabulary.
Avoid repeated catchphrases across prompts. Vary syntax and concrete details while preserving the paired contrast.

cells.instructions: Generate paired money-domain realization prompts. Paper/open means the outcome is visible, expected, pending, held, or not settled. Realized/closed means the same outcome has been sold, paid, received, spent, posted, or finalized. Keep affect and risk preference neutral.
Generate paired non-money finality prompts. Use provisional/visible-but-not-final versus officially posted/submitted/closed framing. Do not use money, casino, wager, investment, or payment language.

Generate paired casino behavior-evaluation prompts. Each prompt should include the scenario plus an instruction to answer with exactly two integers on separate lines: total session wager in CHF from 1 to 1000, then machine risk preference from 1 to 5. Keep the pair identical except that one version leaves the outcome open/on-card/in-play and the other closes it through cash-out/payment/receipt.

Generate paired finance behavior-evaluation prompts. Each prompt should ask for exactly two integers on separate lines: allocation amount from 1 to 1000, then risk preference from 1 to 5. Avoid financial advice language; this is a stylized decision prompt.
